description: Swarm Skills (Skill Mode) — recurring scheduled automations defined as Markdown Skill Cards, run by an in-process orchestrator (agent or command executor), stateful with a git+Markdown audit trail.

userStories.title: Skill Card schema + skills table
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to persist Skill Card definitions so the orchestrator can query what is due.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration 009_skills.sql adds skills table: id, name, project, type, enabled (default 1), frequency, side_effects, timeout, agent, needs_worktree, mcp (json text), command, prompt_ref, on_findings, last_run, next_due, last_status (default 'idle'), file_path, body_hash, updated_at
Export TS types Skill, SkillType ('agent'|'command'), SideEffects ('read-only'|'writes'|'external') from one module
Migration runs cleanly on a fresh DB and on the current v8 DB
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Migration 009_skills.sql + src/skills/types.ts ship; schema tests green; chain verified to v10.

userStories.title: Link agent_runs to skills (reuse, not a new table)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need per-run records so skills are stateful and auditable — reusing the existing agent_runs table instead of a parallel skill_runs table.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration 010_agent_runs_skill_link.sql adds nullable columns to agent_runs: skill_id (FK to skills, ON DELETE SET NULL), finished_at, findings_count
Index on (skill_id, created_at)
Legacy agent_runs inserts (bug_fix, prioritize_backlog) keep working — new columns are nullable
Migration runs cleanly on fresh and current DB
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Run-history implemented by linking the existing agent_runs table (010_agent_runs_skill_link.sql) rather than a new skill_runs table — same goal (per-run records, FK to skills, auditable) with less duplication.

userStories.title: Sync Skill Cards (Markdown to SQLite)
userStories.description: As a user, I want my *.skill.md files to be the source of truth, synced into the DB like other markdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Add sync_skills.ts that reads SWRM_SKILLS_DIR (default ~/.swrm/<project>/skills/), parses frontmatter, upserts into skills
Body content hash stored in body_hash; unchanged files are skipped (no write)
A card with enabled:false is soft-disabled in DB (not deleted)
Unit test: a fixture card parses to the expected row
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/skills/sync.ts (parseSkillCard + syncSkillsDir, dependency-free frontmatter parser). 7 tests green incl. unchanged-skip, body-change update, soft-disable on enabled:false + file removal.

userStories.title: Frequency parser to next_due
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to turn a human frequency string into the next due timestamp.
acceptanceCriteria: Pure function nextDue(frequency, lastRun, now): Date
Supports @hourly, @daily, '@daily HH:MM' (local time), Nh, Nm, weekly:<dow>
Unknown/invalid frequency throws a clear error (no silent fallback)
Unit tests cover each format plus a missed-run (lastRun far in past) case
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/skills/schedule.ts (nextDue, local time per D-2). 11 tests green. Not clamped to future — orchestrator (US-007) collapses catch-up by re-scheduling from now.

userStories.title: Command executor + write-back
userStories.description: As a user, I want a type:command skill to run its shell entrypoint and record the result.
acceptanceCriteria: Runs command in the project cwd, captures stdout/stderr, enforces timeout (kills past it)
Writes a skill_runs row (ok/error) and appends a dated summary to the card ## Runs section (newest first, capped 20)
Non-zero exit sets status='error' with stderr in error; no exception escapes the executor
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/skills/executor.ts (runCommandSkill) + src/skills/runlog.ts (recordRun + appendRunToCard, cap 20). spawn(shell) — command is operator-authored card content. Run history reuses agent_runs (not skill_runs). 5 tests green incl. timeout-kill.

userStories.title: Agent executor (no worktree by default)
userStories.description: As a user, I want a type:agent skill to spawn an agent that reads the card body as its prompt and acts via allowed MCP tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Reuses src/lib/agent_runner.ts; spawns the configured agent (claude/codex/gemini)
No git worktree is created unless needs_worktree:true
Only MCP servers listed in mcp: are passed to the agent run
Captures the agent final summary into the run row and card Runs log
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/skills/agent.ts (runAgentSkill). Reuses agent_runner spawn pattern; card body = prompt; MCP allowlist via SWRM_SKILL_MCP env. No worktree (orchestrator decides cwd). 3 tests green.

userStories.title: In-process orchestrator tick
userStories.description: As a user, I want due skills to run automatically while the swrm server is up.
acceptanceCriteria: On server boot a ticker (default 30s, SWRM_TICK_MS) selects enabled=1 AND next_due<=now AND last_status!='running'
Single-flight: skill marked 'running' before dispatch; on boot any 'running' older than its timeout is reset to 'error' (stale lock)
Missed-run catch-up: a far-past next_due runs once, then next_due is recomputed from now
After each run, last_run, next_due, last_status persisted to DB and written back to the card frontmatter
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/skills/orchestrator.ts (tickOnce, runSkillRow, resetStaleRunning, startOrchestrator). Single-flight claim, catch-up collapse, NULL next_due = due. Wired into server.ts boot. 8 tests green.

userStories.title: Run now manual trigger
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run a skill on demand without waiting for its schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/skills/:id/run enqueues an immediate run (respects single-flight)
Run now button on the skill row triggers it; UI reflects running then ok/error
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: POST /api/skills/:id/run via src/api/skills.ts (runSkillNow → shared runSkillRow). Run-now button in /skills view. E2E verified: returned {status:ok}, card ## Runs written.

userStories.title: Skills list view (/skills)
userStories.description: As a user, I want a screen listing all Skill Cards with status and schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: /skills route renders each card: name, project, type, side_effects badge, enabled state, last_status, last_run, next_due
Topbar nav link added alongside tasks/board
Empty state with guidance when no skills exist
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: src/views/skills.ts (renderSkillsHtml + skillsViewHandler). Nav link added to home/board/tasks/settings topbars. Empty state + XSS-escaped. 7 view tests green; E2E confirmed row renders (name, project, read-only badge).

userStories.title: Enable/pause toggle (writes back to Markdown)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pause a skill from the UI and have it reflected in the source Markdown.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle on the skill row flips enabled in DB and rewrites the card frontmatter enabled: line
A paused skill is skipped by the orchestrator tick
Editing enabled: in the file then syncing yields the same result (file and UI agree)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: setSkillEnabled in src/api/skills.ts: flips DB + rewrites card 'enabled:' line. PATCH /api/skills/:id. E2E verified: pause rewrote card to enabled:false, paused skill skipped by tick. 2 API tests green.

userStories.title: MCP tools for skills
userStories.description: As Claude, I want to list, run, and inspect skills via MCP so I can drive Skill Mode from a conversation.
acceptanceCriteria: Adds mcp__swrm__list_skills, mcp__swrm__run_skill, mcp__swrm__get_skill_runs
Registered in the stdio JSON-RPC server (src/mcp/) and covered by the MCP integration test
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: swrm__list_skills / swrm__run_skill / swrm__get_skill_runs added to src/mcp/tools.ts (client sees mcp__swrm__*). 4 tests green. Note: server registers 'swrm__' prefix per existing convention.

userStories.title: Architecture diagram (diagram-as-code)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a current rendered diagram of Skill Mode so the architecture stays visible.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/skill-mode-architecture.md contains a Mermaid diagram matching the PRD section 6
Diagram renders without syntax errors
Linked from the PRD and the README roadmap entry
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: docs/skill-mode-architecture.md (Mermaid flowchart + module map + invariants). Linked from PRD + README roadmap.
